# Transcrição: The_HIDDEN_Secret_To_Reducing_Your_Nerves!

- **Fonte:** The_HIDDEN_Secret_To_Reducing_Your_Nerves!
- **Duração:** 02:43
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:43]

This public speaking insight took me over a decade to figure out. For the longest time, my students would always ask me, VIN, why do I get so nervous and feel so uncomfortable on stage? And if you've been following my content for a while now, you would already know some of the common reasons. When we worry too much about what other people think of us, it causes us to be more anxious. And when we're scared of failure, it makes us more nervous. But there's one reason I bet you've never thought about. And if you do any form of public speaking, this will be extremely helpful. We all get more nervous and more uncomfortable on stage because of the lights. The lights that are beaming into our eyes, it hinders our ability to concentrate. And if you think about this just for a moment, this is what the police do when they're interrogating someone, to confuse them, right? To rattle them. So this is why with the lights, if you're not used to it, it rattles you when you're on stage, causing you not to give your best performance. So the only way to really start thriving on stage is you need to become comfortable with the lights being aimed directly at you. As a part of my practice as a professional speaker, I used to hire a local theatre, and it's quite cheap where I live, and I would hire it for multiple hours at a time. And I would turn the lights in the theatre on max, and I would give my full presentation, and I would rehearse with the lights aimed directly at me. And I can tell you right now that even when I was in the theatre, rehearsing by myself with the lights, I still felt disorientated. So it took me quite a few goes before I started to get comfortable with the lights in my face. And look, if you want a more budget-friendly way to be able to desensitise yourself to the lights, grab some cheap studio lights and put them up at home. Turn them on to full blast and rehearse in front of them. And if you get comfortable with this, when you step on stage on the day of the presentation, you're going to be able to deliver a much better presentation. Because it won't be abnormal to you, you'll be used to it. When I first started speaking, I did all of the mindset, preparation, I did all of the breathing techniques, but I'd go on stage and I'd still get really nervous. And it was because I overlooked this one thing, the lights. As you become more comfortable with it, because you've rehearsed with the lights on, it's going to help you not just survive your next presentation, it's going to help you thrive. If you are new to my content and public speaking is something you want to get better at, then join me for my free two hour masterclass coming up this February. To save your spot, if you want to join my masterclass, check out the comments section. My team will drop a link there for you to better register. Please make sure you register, spots are limited. Looking forward to seeing you as we kick off 2024 together and you master your communication skills.
